skill.name: vue-router-best-practices
skill.description: Guidance for Vue Router 4 navigation guards, route params, lifecycle behavior, cleanup, and production routing patterns.
skill.summary: Apply Vue Router 4 best practices for safer navigation and route-driven data flows.
skill.author: github.com/vuejs-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found documentation-only Vue Router examples and reference links. The backticks, dynamic imports, hash terms, and URLs are false positives in markdown guidance, with no executable skill code or prompt injection found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static external command alerts point to markdown backticks and Vue Router examples. These snippets are instructional text and are not executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced files are markdown documentation with fenced Vue and JavaScript examples. No Ruby, shell, child process, or command execution path exists in the skill.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Import Examples Are Vue Lazy Loading Guidance
low_findings.description: Static script alerts identify dynamic import calls inside route component examples. They demonstrate Vue Router lazy loading and are not runtime skill scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The import calls appear inside markdown code fences as standard Vue Router route component examples. There is no package script or executable loader in the skill.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: Static network alerts point to public documentation links. The markdown does not fetch, post, or transmit data to these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only URLs are Vue, Vue Router, Vue School, and GitHub reference links. No network API, credential handling, or exfiltration behavior is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Descriptive Text
low_findings.description: Static weak-crypto alerts occur on frontmatter or prose lines about routing behavior. They do not reference cryptographic APIs or algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are YAML metadata or routing descriptions. I found no MD5, SHA1, crypto library calls, password hashing, or encryption implementation.

content.value_statement: Vue Router issues can create stale data, broken redirects, and weak route checks. This skill gives focused patterns for guards, params, cleanup, and production routing.
actual_capabilities: Explains when beforeEnter, beforeEach, beforeRouteEnter, and beforeRouteUpdate run.
Shows patterns for async guard checks, redirects, and error handling.
Describes how route param changes affect component lifecycle hooks.
Covers cleanup for simple hash routing event listeners.
Compares simple routing with Vue Router production setup.
Links each topic to focused reference markdown examples.
limitations: Does not inspect a project automatically or run Vue Router tests.
Does not replace the official Vue Router documentation.
Examples use simplified placeholder functions such as auth and fetch helpers.
Does not cover every Vue Router feature or every server deployment setup.

use_cases.title: Fix Guard Bugs
use_cases.description: Diagnose redirects, missing awaits, and deprecated next calls in Vue Router navigation guards.

use_cases.title: Review Route-Driven Data
use_cases.description: Check whether route param changes reload data through watchers or in-component update guards.

use_cases.title: Plan Production Routing
use_cases.description: Choose Vue Router patterns for lazy loading, route meta, history mode, and cleanup responsibilities.

prompt_templates.title: Identify the Router Issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the vue-router-best-practices skill to explain why my Vue Router navigation behaves incorrectly. Focus on guards, params, and lifecycle timing.

prompt_templates.title: Fix an Auth Guard
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the vue-router-best-practices skill to review my auth guard. Replace deprecated next usage with return-based Vue Router 4 patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Handle Param Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the vue-router-best-practices skill to design a safe pattern for loading data when route params change in a reused component.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Routing Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the vue-router-best-practices skill to audit my SPA routing architecture for redirect loops, async guard failures, cleanup gaps, and production readiness.

output_examples.input: A user detail page shows the previous user after moving from one user route to another.
output_examples.output: The response identifies component reuse as the cause. It recommends watching the route id or using onBeforeRouteUpdate for fresh data.

output_examples.input: An auth guard redirects every unauthenticated navigation to the login route.
output_examples.output: The response explains the redirect loop risk. It recommends excluding public routes or using route meta before returning a login redirect.

output_examples.input: A team wants to replace a simple hash router in a production Vue app.
output_examples.output: The response compares simple routing with Vue Router features. It highlights lazy loading, route meta, history mode, and navigation guards.

best_practices: Use return-based Vue Router 4 guards for redirects, cancellation, and normal navigation.
Handle same-route param changes with watchers or onBeforeRouteUpdate.
Use Vue Router for production SPAs instead of hand-built hash routing.
anti_patterns: Relying on beforeEnter to validate param changes within the same route.
Calling next more than once or forgetting to call it in legacy guards.
Adding hashchange listeners without removing them during component cleanup.

faq.question: What Vue Router version does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Vue Router 4 patterns for Vue 3 applications.

faq.question: Can it write routing code for my app?
faq.answer: It can guide code changes and patterns, but it does not inspect your project automatically.

faq.question: Does beforeEnter run when only route params change?
faq.answer: No. Use onBeforeRouteUpdate or a watcher for same-route param changes.

faq.question: Should I still use next in navigation guards?
faq.answer: Prefer return-based guards in Vue Router 4. Use next only when maintaining legacy code.

faq.question: Does the skill cover redirect loop prevention?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains target route exclusions, route meta checks, and redirect escape patterns.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed static alerts are false positives in markdown examples and documentation links.
